name: cairosvg

metadata.name: CairoSVG
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU Lesser General Public License v3 or later (LGPLv3+)
metadata.summary: A Simple SVG Converter based on Cairo

documentation:
CairoSVG is an SVG converter based on Cairo. It can export SVG files to PDF,
EPS, PS, and PNG files.

* Free software: LGPL license
* For Python 3.7+, tested on CPython and PyPy
* Documentation: https://cairosvg.org/documentation/
* Changelog: https://github.com/Kozea/CairoSVG/releases
* Code, issues, tests: https://github.com/Kozea/CairoSVG
* Code of conduct: https://www.courtbouillon.org/code-of-conduct
* Professional support: https://www.courtbouillon.org
* Donation: https://opencollective.com/courtbouillon

CairoSVG has been created and developed by Kozea (https://kozea.fr).
Professional support, maintenance and community management is provided by
CourtBouillon (https://www.courtbouillon.org).

Copyrights are retained by their contributors, no copyright assignment is
required to contribute to CairoSVG. Unless explicitly stated otherwise, any
contribution intentionally submitted for inclusion is licensed under the LGPL 
license, without any additional terms or conditions. For full
authorship information, see the version control history.

modules.name: cairosvg.helpers
documentation: Surface helpers.

functions.name: cairosvg.helpers.distance
documentation: Get the distance between two points.

functions.name: cairosvg.helpers.paint
documentation:
Extract from value an uri and a color.

See http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG/painting.html#SpecifyingPaint

functions.name: cairosvg.helpers.node_format
documentation:
Return `(width, height, viewbox)` of `node`.

If `reference` is `True`, we can rely on surface size to resolve
percentages.

functions.name: cairosvg.helpers.normalize
documentation: Normalize a string corresponding to an array of various values.

functions.name: cairosvg.helpers.point
documentation: Return `(x, y, trailing_text)` from `string`.

functions.name: cairosvg.helpers.point_angle
documentation: Return angle between x axis and point knowing given center.

functions.name: cairosvg.helpers.preserve_ratio
documentation: Manage the ratio preservation.

functions.name: cairosvg.helpers.clip_marker_box
documentation: Get the clip `(x, y, width, height)` of the marker box.

functions.name: cairosvg.helpers.quadratic_points
documentation: Return the quadratic points to create quadratic curves.

functions.name: cairosvg.helpers.rotate
documentation: Rotate a point of an angle around the origin point.

functions.name: cairosvg.helpers.transform
documentation:
Transform `surface` or `gradient` if supplied using `string`.

See <http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG/coords.html#TransformAttribute>

functions.name: cairosvg.helpers.clip_rect
documentation: Parse the rect value of a clip.

functions.name: cairosvg.helpers.rotations
documentation: Retrieves the original rotations of a `text` or `tspan` node.

functions.name: cairosvg.helpers.pop_rotation
documentation: Removes the rotations of a node that are already used.

functions.name: cairosvg.helpers.zip_letters
documentation:
Returns a list with the current letter's positions (x, y and rotation).

E.g.: for letter 'L' with positions x = 10, y = 20 and rotation = 30:
\>>> \[[10, 20, 30], 'L'\]

Store the last value of each position and pop the first one in order to
avoid setting an x,y or rotation value that have already been used.

functions.name: cairosvg.helpers.flatten
documentation: Flatten the text of a node and its children.

functions.name: cairosvg.helpers.size
documentation:
Replace a `string` with units by a float value.

If `reference` is a float, it is used as reference for percentages. If it
is `'x'`, we use the viewport width as reference. If it is `'y'`, we
use the viewport height as reference. If it is `'xy'`, we use
`(viewport_width ** 2 + viewport_height ** 2) ** .5 / 2 ** .5` as
reference.

classes.name: cairosvg.helpers.PointError
documentation: Exception raised when parsing a point fails.

modules.name: cairosvg.text

functions.name: cairosvg.text.path_length
documentation: Get the length of `path`.

functions.name: cairosvg.text.text

modules.name: cairosvg.url
documentation: Utils dealing with URLs.

functions.name: cairosvg.url.normalize_url
documentation:
Normalize `url` for underlying NT/Unix operating systems.

The input `url` may look like the following:

> - C:Directoryzzz.svg
> - <file://C:Directoryzzz.svg>
> - zzz.svg

The output `url` on NT systems would look like below:

> - <file:///C:/Directory/zzz.svg>

functions.name: cairosvg.url.nt_compatible_path
documentation:
Provide compatible NT file paths for `os.path` functions

`os.path` expects NT paths with no `/` at the beginning. For
example, `/C:/Directory/zzz.svg` would fail `os.path.isfile()`,
`os.path.isdir()` etc. where the expected input for `os.path`
functions is `/C:/Directory/zzz.svg`.

Currently `nt_compatible_path` performs some basic checks and
eliminates the unwanted `/` at the beginning.

functions.name: cairosvg.url.fetch
documentation:
Fetch the content of `url`.

`resource_type` is the mimetype of the resource (currently one of
image/, image/svg+xml, text/css).

functions.name: cairosvg.url.safe_fetch
documentation:
Fetch the content of `url` only if it’s a data-URL.

Otherwise, return an empty SVG.

functions.name: cairosvg.url.parse_url
documentation:
Parse an URL.

The URL can be surrounded by a `url()` string. If `base` is not `None`,
the "folder" part of it is prepended to the URL.

functions.name: cairosvg.url.read_url
documentation:
Get bytes in a parsed `url` using `url_fetcher`.

If `url_fetcher` is None a default (no limitations) URLFetcher is used.

modules.name: cairosvg.bounding_box
documentation:
Calculate bounding box for SVG shapes and paths.

A bounding box is a (minx, miny, width, height) tuple.

functions.name: cairosvg.bounding_box.calculate_bounding_box
documentation:
Calculate `node`'s bounding box.

See <https://www.w3.org/TR/SVG/coords.html#ObjectBoundingBox>

documentation: Get the bounding box of a `polyline` or `polygon` node.

functions.name: cairosvg.bounding_box.bounding_box_path

Get the bounding box of an elliptical arc described by the parameters.

See following website for original code:
http://fridrich.blogspot.nl/2011/06/bounding-box-of-svg-elliptical-arc.html

functions.name: cairosvg.bounding_box.is_valid_bounding_box

functions.name: cairosvg.bounding_box.is_non_empty_bounding_box
documentation: Know whether bounding box is valid and has a size.

modules.name: cairosvg.surface
documentation: Cairo surface creators.

classes.name: cairosvg.surface.Surface

methods.name: cairosvg.surface.Surface.convert
documentation:
Convert an SVG document to the format for this class.

Specify the input by passing one of these:

```
param bytestring

The SVG source as a byte-string.

param file_obj

A file-like object.

param url

A filename.
```

Give some options:

```
param dpi

The ratio between 1 inch and 1 pixel.

param parent_width

The width of the parent container in pixels.

param parent_height

The height of the parent container in pixels.

param scale

The ouptut scaling factor.

param unsafe

A boolean allowing external file access, XML entities
and very large files
(WARNING: vulnerable to XXE attacks and various DoS).
```

Specifiy the output with:

```
param write_to

The filename of file-like object where to write the
output. If None or not provided, return a byte string.
```

Only `bytestring` can be passed as a positional argument, other
parameters are keyword-only.

methods.name: cairosvg.surface.Surface.__init__
documentation:
Create the surface from a filename or a file-like object.

The rendered content is written to `output` which can be a filename,
a file-like object, `None` (render in memory but do not write
anything) or the built-in `bytes` as a marker.

Call the `.finish()` method to make sure that the output is
actually written.

methods.name: cairosvg.surface.Surface.points_per_pixel
documentation: Surface resolution.

methods.name: cairosvg.surface.Surface.device_units_per_user_units
documentation:
Ratio between Cairo device units and user units.

Device units are points for everything but PNG, and pixels for
PNG. User units are pixels.

methods.name: cairosvg.surface.Surface._create_surface
documentation: Create and return `(cairo_surface, width, height)`.

methods.name: cairosvg.surface.Surface.set_context_size
documentation: Set the Cairo context size, set the SVG viewport size.

methods.name: cairosvg.surface.Surface.finish
documentation: Read the surface content.

methods.name: cairosvg.surface.Surface.map_color
documentation: Parse a color `string` and apply `map_rgba` function to it.

methods.name: cairosvg.surface.Surface.draw
documentation: Draw `node` and its children.

Abstract base class for CairoSVG surfaces.

The `width` and `height` attributes are in device units (pixels for
PNG, else points).

The `context_width` and `context_height` attributes are in user units
(i.e. in pixels), they represent the size of the active viewport.

documentation: A surface that writes in PDF format.

classes.name: cairosvg.surface.PSSurface
documentation: A surface that writes in PostScript format.

classes.name: cairosvg.surface.PNGSurface

methods.name: cairosvg.surface.PNGSurface._create_surface
documentation: Create and return `(cairo_surface, width, height)`.

methods.name: cairosvg.surface.PNGSurface.finish

classes.name: cairosvg.surface.SVGSurface
documentation:
A surface that writes in SVG format.

It may seem pointless to render SVG to SVG, but this can be used
with `output=None` to get a vector-based single page cairo surface.

modules.name: cairosvg.parser
documentation: SVG Parser.

functions.name: cairosvg.parser.handle_white_spaces
documentation:
Handle white spaces in text nodes.

See http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG/text.html#WhiteSpace

functions.name: cairosvg.parser.normalize_style_declaration
documentation:
Normalize style declaration consisting of name/value pair.

Names are always case insensitive, make all lowercase.
Values are case insensitive in most cases. Adapt for 'specials':
    id - case sensitive identifier
    class - case sensitive identifier(s)
    font-family - case sensitive name(s)
    font - shorthand in which font-family is case sensitive
    any declaration with url in value - url is case sensitive

functions.name: cairosvg.parser.normalize_noop_style_declaration
documentation:
No-operation for normalization where value is case sensitive.

This is actually the exception to the rule. Normally value will be made
lowercase (see normalize_style_declaration above).

functions.name: cairosvg.parser.normalize_url_style_declaration
documentation:
Normalize style declaration, but keep URL's as-is.

Lowercase everything except for the URL.

functions.name: cairosvg.parser.normalize_font_style_declaration
documentation:
Make first part of font style declaration lowercase (case insensitive).

Lowercase first part of declaration. Only the font name is case sensitive.
The font name is at the end of the declaration and can be 'recognized'
by being preceded by a size or line height. There can actually be multiple
names. So the first part is 'calculated' by selecting everything up to and
including the last valid token followed by a size or line height (both
starting with a number). A valid token is either a size/length or an
identifier.

See http://www.w3.org/TR/css-fonts-3/#font-prop

classes.name: cairosvg.parser.Node

methods.name: cairosvg.parser.Node.__init__

methods.name: cairosvg.parser.Node.text_children
